name: Reid Hoffman
childhood: Reid Garrett Hoffman was born on August 5, 1967, in Palo Alto, California, and grew up in Berkeley, raised by two lawyer parents who were leftist activists. From an early age he was drawn to games and ideas—at just 12 years old he landed his first paid job as an editor at Oakland game company Chaosium. He went on to earn a BS in cognitive science and symbolic systems at Stanford University, then pursued a master's degree in philosophy at Oxford's Wolfson College, graduating in 1993.
childhoodKo: 리드 개럿 호프만은 1967년 8월 5일 캘리포니아주 팔로알토에서 태어나 버클리에서 성장했다. 부모는 모두 변호사이자 진보 성향의 사회운동가였다. 어릴 때부터 게임과 아이디어에 매료되어 불과 12세에 오클랜드의 게임 회사 카오시움에서 첫 유급 일자리를 얻었다. 스탠퍼드 대학교에서 인지과학과 기호 시스템을 전공하고, 이후 옥스퍼드 대학교 울프슨 칼리지에서 철학 석사 학위를 취득했다.

careerTimeline.event: Joined Apple as a software designer, working on eWorld, an early online social network product
careerTimeline.eventKo: 애플에 소프트웨어 디자이너로 입사, 초기 온라인 소셜 네트워크 서비스 'eWorld' 개발에 참여

careerTimeline.event: Founded SocialNet.com, one of the first online matchmaking and social networking websites
careerTimeline.eventKo: 초기 온라인 매치메이킹·소셜 네트워킹 사이트 SocialNet.com 창업

careerTimeline.event: Joined PayPal as COO, becoming a key member of the legendary 'PayPal Mafia' network
careerTimeline.eventKo: 페이팔에 COO로 합류, 훗날 '페이팔 마피아'로 불리는 핵심 인맥 형성

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded LinkedIn in December, envisioning a professional social network to connect the world's workforce
careerTimeline.eventKo: 12월 링크드인 공동 창업, 전 세계 직업인을 연결하는 전문가 소셜 네트워크 비전 제시

careerTimeline.event: LinkedIn officially launched on May 5, quickly growing to hundreds of thousands of professionals
careerTimeline.eventKo: 5월 5일 링크드인 공식 서비스 개시, 빠르게 수십만 명의 전문가 회원 확보

careerTimeline.event: Joined Greylock Partners as a partner, running a $20 million Discovery Fund focused on early-stage investments
careerTimeline.eventKo: 그레이록 파트너스에 파트너로 합류, 초기 스타트업 투자 전담 2,000만 달러 규모의 디스커버리 펀드 운용

careerTimeline.event: LinkedIn went public on the NYSE in May; Hoffman's stake was valued at approximately $2.34 billion at IPO
careerTimeline.eventKo: 링크드인 NYSE 상장(5월), 호프만 보유 지분 가치 약 23억 4,000만 달러 평가

careerTimeline.event: Microsoft acquired LinkedIn for $26.2 billion; Hoffman joined Microsoft's board of directors in 2017
careerTimeline.eventKo: 마이크로소프트가 링크드인을 262억 달러에 인수, 호프만은 2017년 마이크로소프트 이사회에 합류

careerTimeline.event: Launched the Masters of Scale podcast, interviewing iconic entrepreneurs on how great companies grow
careerTimeline.eventKo: '마스터스 오브 스케일' 팟캐스트 출시, 저명한 창업자들과 함께 위대한 기업의 성장 원리를 탐구

failures.title: SocialNet.com Struggles
failures.description: Hoffman's first startup, SocialNet.com (1997), was one of the earliest online social networking sites but failed to gain significant traction. The concept was ahead of its time and the company never reached scale, ultimately shutting down. Hoffman later credited the experience as critical preparation for building LinkedIn.
failures.descriptionKo: 1997년 창업한 첫 스타트업 SocialNet.com은 온라인 소셜 네트워킹의 선구적 시도였지만 유의미한 성장을 이루지 못하고 결국 문을 닫았다. 호프만은 이 실패 경험이 훗날 링크드인을 구축하는 데 결정적인 밑거름이 되었다고 회고한다.

failures.title: Slow LinkedIn Launch
failures.description: In LinkedIn's early years, user growth was painfully slow and the company struggled to convince investors of the business model's viability. During the first year after launch, LinkedIn was adding only about 20 new members per day, raising doubts about whether a professional social network could achieve mass adoption.
failures.descriptionKo: 링크드인 초창기에는 사용자 증가 속도가 매우 느렸다. 서비스 출시 첫 해에는 하루 신규 가입자가 약 20명에 불과했고, 전문가 소셜 네트워크가 대중화될 수 있을지에 대한 회의론이 팽배했다.

quotes.text: An entrepreneur is someone who will jump off a cliff and assemble an airplane on the way down.
quotes.source: Masters of Scale podcast / various interviews

quotes.text: If you are not embarrassed by the first version of your product, you've launched too late.
The fastest way to change yourself is to hang out with people who are already the way you want to be.

books.title: The Start-Up of You
books.description: Co-authored with Ben Casnocha, argues that every individual should manage their career like a startup — adapting, investing in themselves, and building resilient networks.

books.title: The Alliance: Managing Talent in the Networked Age
books.description: Co-authored with Ben Casnocha and Chris Yeh, proposes a new employer-employee compact based on honest, mutual investment rather than traditional job security.

books.title: Blitzscaling: The Lightning-Fast Path to Building Massively Valuable Companies
books.description: Co-authored with Chris Yeh, lays out the strategy of prioritizing speed over efficiency to capture winner-take-all markets, drawing on case studies from Silicon Valley's biggest success stories.
